title: chrysophyllum oliviforme subsp. angustifolium
intro: Chrysophyllum oliviforme subsp. angustifolium is a subspecies of the Chrysophyllum oliviforme tree, also known as the West African star apple. It is native to the tropical regions of West Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Chrysophyllum oliviforme subsp. angustifolium is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and fruits are used in herbal medicine to provide relief from various health issues.
